overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved a site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Drive, and adopted a new salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection districts. Both items passed unanimously with the commission taking steps to protect municipal water supplies and advance public safety infrastructure.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey map and site plan
Planning and development department monthly project update report
Future agenda items and staff updates

discussions.item: Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility site plan
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and municipal court at 3600 Community Center Drive. Chief Jeremy explained the building was rotated to address a 36-inch gas pipeline running through the property, which allowed the building to be placed on higher ground and reduced fill costs. The facility is designed for 50-60 officer capacity for future growth, up from current staffing of 40 officers. Discussion included placement of an alternate storage shed to allow for future expansion northward. A motion was made to recommend approval to the board of trustees subject to staff conditions 2-5. The motion passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
discussions.body: Staff proposed a twice-annual inspection program for salt storage facilities within wellhead protection areas - a warm weather inspection focusing on structural integrity and a winter inspection focusing on operations. The program includes a standardized inspection worksheet similar to DOT standards. Staff noted one existing facility near Sternberg has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and the commission agreed staff should reach out to bring them into compliance. Commissioner Lumper praised the program as well put together. A motion to approve the program with reporting back to both Plan Commission and Public Works passed unanimously.
Minutes from the April 13, 2026 meeting were approved unanimously with no discussion.

Staff announced the first joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Discussion followed about potential residential TIF districts for workforce housing, with staff noting Ehlers has prepared a presentation on qualifications that could be shared with the commission or incorporated into the comprehensive plan process.

publicComment: Jim Penson of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via remote connection. He asked the commission to exercise common sense regarding landscaping requirements for the police department project to avoid excessive taxpayer costs similar to the Green Heck Turner building. He also requested the commission require annual reporting on the salt shed inspection program to ensure complaints don't get lost.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility recommended for approval to Board of Trustees subject to staff conditions
Salt storage shed inspection program approved for implementation with reporting to Plan Commission and Public Works Committee
Staff to reach out to existing salt storage facility on Sternberg that has not gone through wellhead protection conditional use process
First joint steering committee meeting for comprehensive plan scheduled for June 1st at 5:00 p.m.
Staff to send email and calendar invite for steering committee meeting
Electric vehicle charging stations noted as future agenda item for fall
Next regular meeting scheduled for Monday, June 8, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.
title: Plan Commission
